product_overview: A browser-based mini-game site where players enter a name, play two casual games (Find the Admin and Matching Balloons), and compete on a shared leaderboard. The how-to-play pages (PRD pages 1–3) provide rules, tips and a comparison table for both games.
core_goals: Enter a player name and begin playing without signing in
Play 'Find the Admin' to locate 3 hidden heads among 20 circles using the fewest clicks
Play 'Matching Balloons' to match two equal numbers within 5 attempts per row and progress through as many rows as possible
View and compare high scores for both games on the leaderboard
Read rules and tips on the How To Play page before starting a game

features.name: User Onboarding
features.description: Set a player name locally to identify your scores and access the game list from the landing page.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Enter player name -> Submit name -> See player name associated with the session and the list of available games
Navigate to / -> Submit empty name -> See validation prompting to enter a non-empty name

features.name: Find the Admin (Girl Game)
features.description: Play a memory/search minigame where the player reveals circles to find 3 hidden admin heads among 20 circles; the score equals the total number of reveals (fewer is better) and is recorded automatically when the round ends.
user_flows: Navigate to /games/girl -> Start a new round -> See a grid of 20 circles arranged across rows -> Click circles to reveal whether each hides an admin head (each click increments the click count) -> Continue clicking until all 3 admin heads are revealed -> See final score recorded and available on the leaderboard

features.name: Matching Balloons (Balloon Game)
features.description: Play a matching game where each row presents 10 face-down balloons; the player has 5 attempts per row to find two balloons with the same hidden number. Progressed rows survived count as the score and are recorded on the leaderboard.
user_flows: Navigate to /games/balloon -> Start a new row -> Observe a row of 10 face-down balloons -> Click a balloon to reveal its number (consumes one attempt) -> Click another balloon to reveal its number (consumes another attempt) -> If the two revealed numbers match, advance to the next row -> Repeat row play until a row ends after 5 attempts without a match -> See final score recorded as number of rows passed and available on the leaderboard

features.name: Leaderboard
features.description: View and compare high scores for both games; switch between game score lists to see top players and their metrics.
user_flows: Navigate to /leaderboard -> Select the Girl Game scoreboard view -> See top scores and metrics for the Girl Game -> Select the Balloon Game scoreboard view -> See top scores and metrics for the Balloon Game

features.name: How To Play / Instructions
features.description: Read game rules, tips and a side-by-side comparison of both games. The provided PRD pages 1–3 (images and text) show step-by-step instructions: Girl Game — 20 circles, find 3 heads; Balloon Game — 10 balloons per row, 5 attempts per row; plus play tips and a summary table of scoring rules.
user_flows: Navigate to /howtoplay -> Read the step-by-step instructions and tips for both games (see PRD pages 1–3 for images and comparison table) -> Choose which game to play by navigating to /games/girl or /games/balloon

routes.description: Landing page to enter player name and choose games
Leaderboard page to view high scores
Instructions on how to play the games

features.name: User Onboarding
features.description: Enter player name to start playing games
user_interactions: Click submit button to set player name

features.name: Girl Game Play
features.description: Play the Girl Game by clicking circles to find admins

features.name: Balloon Game Play
features.description: Play Balloon game by clicking balloons to find matching numbers

features.name: Leaderboard
user_interactions: Toggle between Girl Game and Balloon Game tabs to view scores

known_limitations.impact: Admin dashboard route only renders a static placeholder
